332.reason: [If ch is not '&', this appends the wrong character (e.g., EOF or another symbol) to content, and then proceeds to create an AND token incorrectly. The token assignment after the if block should only happen when ch == '&'.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.25 , reason:  [When a single '&' is encountered, an error is reported but execution continues, appending the next character (which may be anything) and creating an AND token incorrectly]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.25 , reason:  [When ch is not '&', this appends the wrong character (e.g., EOF or another symbol) to content, and then overwrites the ERROR token with an AND token]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.5 , reason:  [If ch is not '&', this appends the wrong character (e.g., EOF or another symbol) to content, and then proceeds to create an AND token incorrectly. The token assignment after the if block should only happen when ch == '&'.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.25 , reason:  [This line executes even in the error case, overwriting the ERROR token with invalid content]

333.reason: [This line should not execute when a single '&' error occurs; it incorrectly creates an AND token even for invalid single '&']

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.25 , reason:  [This line should not execute when a single '&' error occurs; it incorrectly creates an AND token even for invalid single '&']
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.25 , reason:  [This line incorrectly creates an AND token even when only a single '&' was found, overriding the previously set ERROR token]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.25 , reason:  [This overwrites the previously set ERROR token with an AND token even when only a single '&' was found]

342.reason: [Same issue as above: if ch is not '|', this line still appends it and then creates an OR token, which is incorrect. The token creation should only occur in the success case.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.5 , reason:  [Same issue as above: if ch is not '|', this line still appends it and then creates an OR token, which is incorrect. The token creation should only occur in the success case.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.25 , reason:  [This line executes even in the error case, overwriting the ERROR token with invalid content]

317.reason: [The condition `ch != EOF` is evaluated after `ch == '\n'`, but if `ch` is EOF, the first condition fails and EOF is not handled. Also, when encountering EOF in a line comment, the loop never exits, causing an infinite loop or undefined behavior. Additionally, after reading '\n', it should increment `_lineno` before recursing.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 1.0 , reason:  [The condition `ch != EOF` is evaluated after `ch == '\n'`, but if `ch` is EOF, the first condition fails and EOF is not handled. Also, when encountering EOF in a line comment, the loop never exits, causing an infinite loop or undefined behavior. Additionally, after reading '\n', it should increment `_lineno` before recursing.]
